steps.title: Vertex form gives the minimum instantly
steps.content: $f(x) = (x-4)^2 + 6$ is in vertex form $(x-h)^2 + k$ with vertex $(4, 6)$. Since the leading coefficient is positive the parabola opens up, so the vertex is the minimum.

steps.title: Read off the minimum value
steps.content:
The minimum value is $k = 6$, achieved at $x = 4$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
